name: Freddie Keppard
abstract: Freddie Keppard (sometimes rendered as Freddy Keppard; February 27, 1890 – July 15, 1933) was an American jazz cornetist who once held the title of "King" in the New Orleans jazz scene. This title was previously held by Buddy Bolden and succeeded by Joe Oliver.

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph Ralph Robinson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Do you remember any of the other bands when you were younger? Did you ever hear Freddie Keppard? No. That you remember? [INTERVIEWEE] No.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Wasn’t that the story with Freddie Keppard? ... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Wouldn’t record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Celestine had his brass band. Freddie Keppard. Freddie Keppard had his brass band.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Dawson
about_quotes.evidence: I played with him a long time ago when he was playing violin... Freddie would pick it up... he worked there with us then from that on.

about_quotes.speaker: Charles "Creole Charlie" Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I know the next year, I was with, uh, Clarinet King at Harmon Dreamland, and we had Freddie Keppard with us.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Gabriel
about_quotes.evidence: I have never heard too much of the word jazz until later on after I got to be around 14 or 15... from what I can understand Freddie Keppard was a member of my grandfather’s band... So I didn’t hear nothing about the word jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charlie Gabriel interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 9, 2020
